Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from {{ill|Izumi Station (Fukushima)|ja|泉駅 (福島県いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Suwa Hachiman Shrine |image= [[File:Suwahachiman.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall |location= {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. It served as the '''prayer shrine''' (祈願社) of the former '''Izumi Domain''' and held the historical Rank of '''Gōsha''' (郷社). The current shrine grounds are traditionally considered the '''former residence site''' of the '''Iwaki (Ibaraki) Court Officer, Taira no Masauji''', from the '''Heian period'''. Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from {{ill|Izumi Station (Fukushima)|ja|泉駅 (福島県いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from {{ill|Izumi Station (Fukushima)|ja|泉駅 (福島県いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Suwa Hachiman Shrine |image= [[File:Suwahachiman.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall |location= {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|ja|福島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|ja|いわき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. It served as the '''prayer shrine''' (祈願社) of the former '''Izumi Domain''' and held the historical Rank of '''Gōsha''' (郷社). The current shrine grounds are traditionally considered the '''former residence site''' of the '''Iwaki (Ibaraki) Court Officer, Taira no Masauji''', from the '''Heian period'''. Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from {{ill|Izumi Station (Fukushima)|ja|泉駅 (福島県いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市)]] In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from {{ill|Izumi Station (Fukushima)|ja|泉駅 (福島県いわき市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumi Station (Fukushima)|qq=}} * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市)]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Suwa Hachiman Shrine |image= [[File:Suwahachiman.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall |location= [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. It served as the '''prayer shrine''' (祈願社) of the former '''Izumi Domain''' and held the historical Rank of '''Gōsha''' (郷社). The current shrine grounds are traditionally considered the '''former residence site''' of the '''Iwaki (Ibaraki) Court Officer, Taira no Masauji''', from the '''Heian period'''. Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from [[:en:Izumi Station (Iwaki)|Izumi Station (Fukushima)]] * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市)]]

{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Suwa Hachiman Shrine |image= [[File:Suwahachiman.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall |location= [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from [[:en:Izumi Station (Iwaki)|Izumi Station (Fukushima)]] * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] {{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Suwa Hachiman Shrine |image= [[File:Suwahachiman.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall |location= [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. It served as the '''prayer shrine''' (祈願社) of the former '''Izumi Domain''' and held the historical Rank of '''Gōsha''' (郷社). The current shrine grounds are traditionally considered the '''former residence site''' of the '''Iwaki (Ibaraki) Court Officer, Taira no Masauji''', from the '''Heian period'''. Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from [[:en:Izumi Station (Iwaki)|Izumi Station (Fukushima)]] * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:いわき市の建築物]] [[Category:いわき市の歴史]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]]

IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from [[:en:Izumi Station (Iwaki)|Izumi Station (Fukushima)]] * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Suwa Hachiman Shrine |image= [[File:Suwahachiman.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall |location= [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. It served as the '''prayer shrine''' (祈願社) of the former '''Izumi Domain''' and held the historical Rank of '''Gōsha''' (郷社). The current shrine grounds are traditionally considered the '''former residence site''' of the '''Iwaki (Ibaraki) Court Officer, Taira no Masauji''', from the '''Heian period'''. Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from [[:en:Izumi Station (Iwaki)|Izumi Station (Fukushima)]] * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)|version=99765382|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]][[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:likely untranslated pages]] }} [[sn:Suwa Hachiman Shrine (Izumicho, Iwaki City)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市泉町)]] [[ja:諏訪八幡神社 (いわき市)]] [[Category:Buildings and structures completed in the 17th century]] [[Category:9th-century establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Fukushima Prefecture]]

Worship Hall |location= [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to いわき湯本インターチェンジ}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from [[:en:Izumi Station (Iwaki)|Izumi Station (Fukushima)]] * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |name= Suwa Hachiman Shrine |image= [[File:Suwahachiman.jpg|270px]]
Worship Hall |location= [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], Izumi-machi 6-chome 10-17 |coordinates= {{coord|36|56|47|N|140|51|28|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} |sacred_objects= |shrine_Rank= Gōsha (郷社) |established= '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE) |main_hall= '''Irimoya-zukuri''', copper roofing |alternate_names= Izumi Suwa Shrine |annual_festival= {{Plainlist}} * January 1 – '''Saitan-sai''' (New Year’s Festival) * January 8 – '''Kiyoharai''' (Purification Ceremony) * February 11 – '''Kenkoku-sai''' (National Foundation Day Festival) * May 5 – '''Spring Grand Festival''' * Day before '''Respect for the Aged Day (September)''' – '''Summer Festival''' * Second Sunday of October – '''Autumn Grand Festival''' * November 30 – '''Niiname-sai''' (Harvest Festival) {{Endplainlist}} |ceremonies= '''Mikoshi Togyo''', '''Yabusame''' (Horseback Archery) }} '''Suwa Hachiman Shrine''' (諏訪八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in '''Izumi-machi, Iwaki, Fukushima Prefecture'''. It served as the '''prayer shrine''' (祈願社) of the former '''Izumi Domain''' and held the historical Rank of '''Gōsha''' (郷社). The current shrine grounds are traditionally considered the '''former residence site''' of the '''Iwaki (Ibaraki) Court Officer, Taira no Masauji''', from the '''Heian period'''. Some historical sources also identify the site as '''Takijiri Castle''', which was ruled by the '''Ōyama clan''' during the medieval period.Takijiri Castle’s location has been debated; some sources identify it as the shrine grounds (Hirajiro 平城), while others suggest it was on a hill in Izumi-machi Hon’ya (Yamajiro 山城). However, archaeological surveys conducted between 1993 and 1994 in the hill area found no castle structures. See: Iwaki City Board of Education, "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37, Takijiri Castle Site A." == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|健御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Hondawake no Mikoto|ja|誉田別命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} (Emperor Ōjin, the 15th Emperor of Japan) == History == According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', enshrined '''Suwa Myōjin''' at '''Kameishi-hara (亀石ヶ原, modern Izumi-machi Takijiri Kameishi)''' during the '''Daidō era''' (806–810 CE). During the '''Tenbun era''' (1532–1555), a '''great tsunami''' destroyed the original shrine buildings, leading to its relocation '''to Takijiri Hill'''.Modern location: Iwaki City, Izumi-machi Takijiri. This is at the southernmost part of the former village, where the Kamado River meets the Fujiwara River. See {{Kotobank|1=滝尻村|2=日本歴史地名大系}}. In '''1634''' (Kanei 11), the '''Izumi Domain''' was established as a branch of the '''Iwakitaira Domain''', with its '''first daimyō''', '''Naitō Masaharu''', designating Suwa Hachiman Shrine as the domain’s '''prayer shrine'''. In '''1675''' (Enpō 3), the '''second daimyō''', '''Naitō Masachika''', relocated the shrine to its current location, considered the former '''residence site of Taira no Masauji'''. Subsequent domain lords, including the '''Itakura clan''' (from 1702) and the '''Honda clan''' (from 1746), continued to revere the shrine, providing land grants and ceremonial funds. In '''1793''' (Kansei 5), the '''second Honda daimyō, Honda Tadatada''', funded the construction of the current '''main hall'''. In '''1873''' (Meiji 6), the shrine was '''officially recognized as a Gōsha (district shrine)''', merging the worship of '''Suwa Myōjin and Hachiman Daibosatsu'''. In '''2019''' (Reiwa 1), in celebration of '''the enthronement of Emperor Naruhito''', '''1,200 years of enshrinement''', and '''recovery from the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake''', extensive renovations were undertaken, including '''restorations of the main hall, rebuilding of the grand torii gate, and a new hand-washing station (temizuya)'''. == Shrine facilitiesや祭事 == File:Suwajinjaato.jpg|Suwa Shrine Former Site Marker File:Akibajinja01.jpg|Akiha Shrine (境内社) File:Takijirijoshi.jpg|Monument for Takijiri Castle Site and '''Anju & Zushio''' Folklore File:Dorui.jpg|Takijiri Castle Earthworks File:Takijiribosasara.jpg|'''Takijiri Bōsasara Dance''' during the Summer Festival"Bō" refers to '''staff martial arts''', while "sasara" refers to a '''percussive ritual tool'''. The dance has connections to the '''samurai arts of Tsukahara Bokuden'''. See: [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara]. File:流鏑馬神事 いわき市諏訪八幡神社.jpg|'''Yabusame (Horseback Archery)''' at the Autumn Grand Festival File:泉藩士「松井秀簡」顕彰碑.jpg|Memorial for '''Matsui Shūkan''', a samurai of Izumi DomainMatsui Shūkan was a samurai of the '''Izumi Domain''' who opposed war during the '''Boshin War''' and committed ritual suicide in protest. See {{Kotobank|1=松井秀簡|2=デジタル版日本人名大辞典＋Plus}}. File:「亀石」諏訪八幡神社の元宮跡地.jpg|'''Kameishi (亀石)''' – Former Shrine Site (境外社) == 摂社末社 == Includes: - Suwa Shrine - Wakagi Shrine - Akiha Shrine - Hankansha Shrine - Inari Shrine - Ashio Shrine - Kotohira Shrine - Memorial for '''Izumi District’s war dead''' - Kameishi (former shrine site) == 歴史的背景 == The '''Izumi-machi Takijiri district''' of Iwaki, where Suwa Hachiman Shrine is located, was historically a '''port town''' and '''postal station''' for goods transportation. According to shrine tradition, '''Taira no Masauji''', an official of '''Iwaki Province''', originally built his residence here and enshrined Suwa Myōjin. Archaeological surveys have confirmed that this area was a '''thriving settlement during the Nara and Heian periods'''.See: {{Cite web |title=The Ritual Tools of Heian Iwaki |url=https://note.com/tunawataridori/n/n7994e95e6662 |website=note（ノート） |date=2020-05-19 |access-date=2023-09-28}} During the '''Nanboku-chō period''', in '''1337''', the Southern Court-aligned '''Ōyama clan''' was defeated by the Northern Court-aligned '''Ishikawa clan''' and '''Iga Morimitsu'''.See: {{Cite web |title=Iino Family Documents |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/134264 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-09-28}} == Access == * '''Car:''' ~15 minutes from {{ill|Iwaki-Yumoto IC|ja|いわき湯本IC|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaki-Yumoto IC|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to いわき湯本インターチェンジ}} * '''Taxi:''' ~4 minutes from [[:en:Izumi Station (Iwaki)|Izumi Station (Fukushima)]] * '''Walking:''' ~17 minutes from Izumi Station * '''Bus:''' Izumi Station South Exit → Get off at '''Izumi Rokuchōme''' stop (4-min walk) == References == == 参考文献 == * "Iwaki City Archaeological Report No. 37" (Iwaki City Board of Education, 1994) * "Fukushima Prefectural Cultural Heritage Reports" == External Links == * [https://www.pref.fukushima.lg.jp/site/movie-now/ch-furusato2021-bousasara.html Fukushima Prefecture Official Video on Bōsasara] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]]
